title: Osteomeles anthyllidifolia
intro: Osteomeles anthyllidifolia, also known as the ma'o hau hele, is a small flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Osteomeles anthyllidifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often prepared into a tea or infused oil, and is also used in poultices to treat skin conditions, while also being used in traditional Hawaiian ceremonies for its spiritual significance.
